canonical_definition: RAID is consistently characterized across the provided sources as a dataset for evaluating machine-generated text detection. The prevailing description is that it is a multi-domain benchmark, with one paper specifically using its "Abstracts" and "Wiki" domains for evaluation. Its application for "cross-domain evaluation" is a stated purpose, and one study uses it as an "out-of-domain" dataset for this reason. A more detailed definition from one source specifies that RAID also includes adversarial examples and covers text from multiple models. In practice, researchers use RAID to evaluate their detection methods, often in conjunction with other datasets such as M4, MAGE, and RealDet.
papers: Enhancing Interpretable Image Classification ThroughLLMAgents and Conditional Concept Bottleneck Models
Multi-document Summarization through Multi-document Event Relation Graph Reasoning inLLMs: a case study in Framing Bias Mitigation
Deep Kernel Relative Test for Machine-generated Text Detection
PaLD: Detection of Text Partially Written by Large Language Models

definitions.text: A dataset with multiple domains, including Abstracts and Wiki, used for cross-domain evaluation of LLM text detection.
definitions.paper: PaLD: Detection of Text Partially Written by Large Language Models
